methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
EnerVenue, a Series B-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Yi Cui, has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public and 8 private—in the same sector and development stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total raised ($400M) as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression, implies a valuation of $760M. The peer-median method across the 6-company comparison set yields $355M; the broader peer universe spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting material dispersion within the climate tech sector at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $302M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $558M. The wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty driven by peer heterogeneity—Commonwealth Fusion Systems trades at a significant premium while others cluster in the $300M–$500M band. Key sensitivities include EnerVenue's relative technical differentiation within energy storage, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term path to revenue or deployment validation.
